label: Discrete Mathematics
shortDescription: Logic, sets, proofs, combinatorics, graphs, and number theory — the mathematical foundation of computer science.
modulesDescription: Read the discrete mathematics chapters in order, or jump directly to the topic you need.
ogTitle: Learn Discrete Mathematics for Free | CalcPath

topics.title: Logic and Proofs
topics.description: Propositions, truth tables, logical connectives, direct proofs, contradiction, and mathematical induction.

topics.title: Sets and Functions
topics.description: Set operations, Venn diagrams, functions, injectivity, surjectivity, bijections, and power sets.

topics.title: Relations and Equivalence
topics.description: Binary relations, equivalence relations, partial orders, total orders, and posets.

topics.title: Combinatorics
topics.description: Counting principles, permutations, combinations, binomial coefficients, pigeonhole principle, and inclusion-exclusion.

topics.title: Graph Theory
topics.description: Graphs, vertices, edges, paths, cycles, trees, bipartite graphs, and Eulerian paths.

topics.title: Number Theory
topics.description: Primes, divisors, Euclidean algorithm, modular arithmetic, and the Chinese remainder theorem.
